paragraph.content: propane gas, or liquefied petroleum gas, is an essential energy commodity providing heat, hot water, cooking fuel, and motor fuel among its many uses to millions of Americans;
the use of propane is especially important to rural citizens and farmers, offering an efficient and economical source of gas energy;
propane has been recognized as a clean fuel and can contribute in many ways to reducing the pollution in our cities and towns; and
propane is primarily domestically produced and its use provides energy security and jobs for Americans.

note.heading: Short Title of 2014 Amendment
note.p: Pub. L. 113–269, § 1, Dec. 18, 2014, 128 Stat. 2947, provided that: “This Act [amending sections 6404 and 6408 of this title] may be cited as the ‘Propane Education and Research Enhancement Act of 2014’.”

note.heading: Short Title
note.p: Pub. L. 104–284, § 1, Oct. 11, 1996, 110 Stat. 3370, provided that: “This Act [enacting this chapter] may be cited as the ‘Propane Education and Research Act of 1996’.”
